pages.text: Entrepreneurship Support to Women and SC & ST Communities Stand-up India Scheme facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore Apply Here 299274 Total Applications Total Amount 275321 Sanctioned Applications Sanctioned Amount 24613 Handholding Agencies 4663 HHA Requests 77 Lenders Onboarded 156312 Branches Connected What is Stand-Up India? Stand-Up India Scheme Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise. This enterprise may be in manufacturing, services, agri-allied activities or the trading sector. In case of non-individual enterprises at least 51% of the shareholding and controlling stake should be held by either an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur. Salient Features Borrower Type Loans to SC/ST and women entrepreneurs above 18 years. Enterprise Loans for setting up greenfield enterprise. Loan size Loans between ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore. Nature of Loan Composite loan (inclusive of Term Loan and Working Capital). Promoter Contribution 15% Margin money. Security Security cover under Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Stand-Up India Loans (CGFSIL). Key Elements Stand-Up India Ecosystem How to Apply Impact Stories POONGOTHAI S K. TAMIL NADU / CHENNAI Vision and Optics Pursuing Entrepreneurial Pursuits Ms Poongodhai is the beneficiary under STAND UP INDIA loan scheme of Rs.10.50 lakh. She has been given loan for setting up of Optometric clinic with vision therapy as she is a low vision therapist as well as OPTICAL GALLERY at Ashok Nagar Chennai. The loan has been sanctioned by PNB, Anna Nagar Branch, Chennai. The account is regular. Gaining experience from her father in law, the promoter has become a specialist in low vision therapy and is happy that she is able to STAND ALONE as an optometrist. There is an OPTICAL GALLERY along with her OPTOMETRIC CLINIC which has sheet magnifiers, hand magnifiers, telescope, illuminated magnifiers, scanner – all to improve the vision and correct the vision through these magnifiers and therapy. The promoter has seen the website and learnt about the Anna Nagar branch extending STAND UP INDIA scheme and approached directly. According to the promoter, she never dreamt of getting the loan sanctioned in shortest time and is very happy and able to earn Rs.1 lakh per month from the Optical Gallery cum Optometric Clinic, which has vision therapy—as a low vision specialist the promoter has utmost satisfaction of serving the society As the Bank is empowering the women by various schemes, the promoter is fully committed to the Bank to repay the dues promptly and thereafter she can even demand further finance when she goes for further expansion-- the full satisfaction of being SELF EMPLOYED AND SELF DEPENDANT. Read More Sharma Traders. HIMACHAL PRADESH / KANGRA Manufacturing Self Sustenance Mrs Vandana Sharma w/o Shri Vasu Sharma is graduate having done degree in Arts. She got the opportunity to serve as teacher in the reputed Private School. But she refused the offer as she was not interested in salaried Job. Instead she was interested in carrying on her own business by setting up new firm. After Launching of the scheme she approached the bank and inquired all about stand Up India scheme, whether she is eligible for credit assistance under the scheme. The Branch explained the benefits of Stand Up India scheme and motivated her to take advantage of the Government Scheme, with the help of Stand Up India Loan of Rs.10 Lakh and her own savings, she established the firm in the name of M/s. Sharma Traders. She has started the business of disposable goods (Plates, glass, bowls, spoons etc.) and taken the agency of Safal Products. After few months the business rotation is increasing day by day. She has given the employment to three persons. Now the unit is supplying the goods in and around dharamshala on retail basis as well as supplying in bulk to corporate clients. With her hard work and dedication she has spread her business to all near by areas and turnover is increasing since last 6 months. Mrs Vandana Sharma feels without Stand Up India Scheme, she would not have got this much confidence to turn into a new age entrepreneur and is grateful to the bank for guiding her to apply loan under Stand Up India. she is happy with the increased living standard of her family and good earning. Read More ODHINI BOUTIQUE. UTTAR PRADESH / Saharanpur Textile and Dyeing Hard Work to Success Miss Parvinder kaur living in Gurudwara Road, Saharanpur aged 46, is hard working woman and was keen interest in designing and embroidery work and wanted to doing business as boutique, for the business establishment she was not so confidant without financial support, she tried and contacted many banks for loans but was of no avail, the banks were asking for securities/guarantees, which was very discouraging factors, for which she was very depressed and due to theses reasons she could not establish her own business. After few months, Stand Up India was launched, she visited Punjab & Sind Bank, Ambala Road Saharanpur branch and met branch Manager, who very positively explained her about the scheme and motivated her to take advantage of the scheme specially for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs. As per eligibility she applied for a loan of Rs. 10 lakh and sanctioned the same by branch, with the help of the scheme Miss Parvinder Kaur was able to establish a dream firm in name and style of M/s ODHINI BOUTIQUE. The promoter established the firm and the firm is doing good sales and also she has employed 8 persons. From this she improved her lifestyle. From the firm she feels her sale is increasing day by day and will cross Rs.20000/- to Rs.25000/- per day in near future. Read More RAYS INFRA. GUJARAT / BHAVNAGAR Manufacture of hume pipes and other pre-fabricated structures Transforming Lives Loan for the new entrepreneur and especially where the woman is the Proprietor was the biggest challenge. The customer had applied directly under the ‘STAND UP INDIA’ through IDBI Bank, Bhavnagar Branch. The enterprise is manufacturing of PSC Cement Poles (Use for Electric Line Erection). The customer had applied for all kinds of Statutory Registration and made the production unit efficient, well equipped and well Advanced were the major challenges faced. The challenges were overcome successfully from Term Loan from Banker. The customer has applied for CLCSS Subsidy which is under process. The enterprise has employed many skilled & unskilled labor along with Clerical & Supervising staff will get the direct employment and many raw material supplier and Transporters will get the indirect employment. Within two and half month of manufacturing, the unit has registered a turnover of Rs.38.68 lakh and also got approved from the Govt. of Gujarat and also studying the demand of Cement articles. The customer has applied for quality certificate (ISO: 9001) for its product. Read More R.K.Garments. WEST BENGAL / SOUTH 24 PARGANAS Manufacture of all types of textile garments and clothing accessories Being Economically Independent R K Garments, at Village-Chaulkhola is owned by Smt. Taslima Bibi w/o Mojaffar Jamadar. They are engaged in manufacturing and trading of jeans. Her husband had more than 10 years experience of working in Jeans manufacturing enterprise. The promoter approached Bank with a dream to start her own business. The promoter has started the business in her own brand namely-R K Life and has launched it in the market that comprises of ABM Hat at Metiabruz, which happens to be Asia’s largest wholesale market of Jeans. Besides, the products are also marketed outside of West Bengal like Bihar Jharkhand and Orissa. The promoter is economically independent now. and has created employment generation for many. As of now, 25 labourers are engaged in the unit. It is not only feeding the family but has provided bread and butter to 25 families. Stand Up India Scheme is truly a catalyst for economic development of our nation. Mrs Taslima Bibi’s market knowledge, her sense of business and her plans for starting her own brand were quite impressive. Bank sanctioned a loan of Rs.10.20 lakh under Stand up India with guarantee coverage and the husband of the promoter Mr Mojaffar Jamadar extended all help to get an idea about the business. The promoter got the trade license and submitted all the requisite documents to Bank. Read More M/s suryalok technologies. TELANGANA / RANGA REDDY Manufacture of bearings, and gears, Gearing up for Success Mrs S Vasavi, had plans to start a new unit in the name of M/s Suryalok Technologies. She proposed to purchase a CNC machine for manufacturing of bearings, gears etc. She approached the Branch for a loan to purchase the machine and set up the enterprise. She has employed 3 staff members to undertake the work. Read More samina yasmin. UTTAR PRADESH / Varanasi Fashion design related to textiles,shoes, jewelry and furniture Facilitating shift from Hand to Tech Mrs. Samin Yasim, aged about 23 years from Minority Community, a new women entrepreneur approached the Bank for setting up a unit where computerized embroidery machines can be installed. Before the installation of Computerized Embroidery Machines, she was doing embroidery by hand and not able to get sufficient income (due to time taken for embroidery work). She did not have sufficient capital to buy the computerized embroidery machines and approached the bank for the finance on the basis of information collected through visual media. Though starting up a new venture invites many hardships which was lessened by availing a term loan and overdraft under the government’s new scheme Stand Up India though Dena Bank. The bank supported the Greenfield venture by sanctioning loan on the basis of the experience of the promoters. As Varanasi is known for its Banarasi saree worldwide, setting up such unit would enable the entrepreneur to excel well in this field. The automatic embroidery machines purchased out of the bank finance would enable the raw saree/ textile material to have the desired embroidery done on it; after which final saree is ready to supply. Promoter contribution is through its own sources. Employments are generated directly to the skilled and unskilled laborer. The unit has employed ample of unskilled as well as skilled laborers. The unit is getting regular orders from the different manufacturers for the embroidery on sarees. The unit has targeted to achieve a net profit of 1.69 lakh and sales of 31.20 lakh in the six month, after its establishment. The entrepreneur get’s the orders as job works. The ready material is marketed locally and it is planned to supply it to other parts of the state and country. Read More Yamuna Punch & Pack.. GUJARAT / VALSAD Packaging Employee to Entrepreneur Mr. Ajay Kumar, a rural youth of 26 years, did not compete his 12th standard. Beaten by poverty and continuous distress, he had lost hope for better life. He joined a packaging industries as a Production Supervisor for seven years on a meager salary for Rs.12,000/- per month. But he wanted to be independent and had dreams to start his own venture. In the meantime, Govt. has announced Stand up India Scheme for SC/ST and Women beneficiaries and he could think of being an entrepreneur. He had undergone a training in entrepreneurship development. The training inputs indeed provided an effective platform for Ajay to know more about entrepreneurship. The training programme was turning point in his life which helped him to start a unit for manufacturing fancy corrugated box for which he took a loan of from Dena Bank, Valsad branch under Stand up India Scheme. Shri Ajay has provided employment opportunity to 21 persons. The monthly turnover of the firm is Rs.17.00 lakh. He is purchasing row material of corrugated sheet @ Rs.26 per kg. then making of corrugated Box. The selling rate of corrugated box is Rs.34 per kg in this scenario net profit after conversion is around Rs.2 per kg. He has reached monthly production of 50 ton of corrugated boxes and is getting net profit of Rs.1,00,000 per month. In need of regular income to feed his family, he has applied for purchase of Machinery and raw materials for cartons for packaging loan at Dena Bank Valsad Branch under Stand up India scheme. Read More FUNRALLY GAMES PVT LTD. DELHI / EAST Publishing of computer games for all platforms Differentiator In 1991, at the age of 15, Siddhant Paswan became Siddhant Kumar, to avoid the shadow of caste discrimination. Kumar’s journey began with painting signboards, election banners, school walls. He enrolled in Jamia Millia Islamia University’s (Faculty Of Fine Arts) and later completed his masters in design from IIT Bombay. Kumar studied by day and painted walls and ceilings at night—earning Rs. 200 per day. With a friend from NIT, Bhopal, and another from NIFT, Chennai, Kumar founded FunRally Games. The company designs board games, card games, dice-based games and miniature war games —mostly exploring how to use different concepts to make instructional games and reinvent the traditional games from villages. Further they are into manufacturing and selling of daily used and household articles with denim decoring. “For initial years, we had absolutely no money,” he says. There were a few months when they had to stop working because they fell short of funds, by as little as Rs.10,000/-. “We had used every penny we had. Our parents’, our own money, everything was in it,” says Kumar. “While entrepreneurs in India face obstacles because of lack of credit from the formal banking system, potential Dalit entrepreneurs are doubly handicapped because they almost invariably lack the collateral and also because of their more limited access to informal credit through community networks. However with the launch of ‘Stand Up India’ he approached Vijaya Bank,Patparganj Branch for a bank finance of term loan of Rs. 25.00 Lakh for setting up Machineries like Sewing Machines, Computer Systems, Commercial Vehicle, etc. and also a working capital of Rs. 25.00 Lakh for day to day operations. On getting timely financial support, they have now set up their business and are accepting more and more orders. Kumar’s FunRally Games employs seven people and have projected an ambitious sales of Rs. 2 crore in this financial year. Even now when Kumar goes back home, he looks at the electric pole near his house with a banner he had painted as a teenager, directing everyone to the salon next door. And every time he looks at it, it strikes him that had he not got all financial supports, he would probably have still been the man with a paintbrush, painting people’s houses. Read More steel point. ASSAM / DHUBRI Mfg of Steel Products Homemaker Turns Entrepreneur Mrs. Manasi Devi, a small businesswoman in Dhubri town thought of starting a new business. The idea was quite new to the market and could fetch her better income. Keeping that point of view, she, with the help of her family savings, set up a factory for steel fabrication and manufacturing of steel products. Under the Stand up India scheme, the Bank financed Mrs. Devi Rs 15 lakh (Rs 13 lakh as working capital and Rs 2 lakh as for purchase of machinery). It helped her to set up “STEEL POINT” at prime location of Dhubri Town giving direct employment to 15 people at a time. Presently the turnover of the store is around 5000/- to 7000/- per day with approx 3-4 work order per day. She has also started receiving enquiries/ work order from various govt. organizations, schools etc. She has also started engaging labour for manufacturing of steel products as per order in the work site. With her hard work and good marketing skills she is poised to grow. Stand up India has helped her to take a leap in following her dreams of expanding her business and providing employment to other people. She has plan to go for quality certificates. Read More ALLURE FEMALE BEAUTY LOUNGE. UTTAR PRADESH / Lucknow Beauty Parlour Emergence of Woman Entrepreneurship Sangeeta Tandon was a retired executive engineer from UPPCL. After her retirement, she wanted to indulge herself in some activities through which she can utilize her spare time while giving employment to needy ladies of her society. As she was the regular customer of Punjab and Sind Bank, she approached the Bank with an idea of opening a Beauty Parlour. The manager of the branch welcomed her ideas and considered a loan under Stand Up India scheme. She was sanctioned a loan of Rs 10 lakh in the name and style of ALLURE FEMALE BEAUTY LOUNGE. She has employed to 7-8 ladies of her area who are now earning Rs 5000/- per month and are contributing to their families' wellbeing. Read More Purbanchal Concrete. WEST BENGAL / COOCH BEHAR Fly ash bricks Pooling Entrepreneurial Dreams Together This is a unit started as partnership firm by 3 women. They had approached the bank for setting up of a unit fly ash and bricks manufacturing. They got full support from their family members and the bank. They were sanctioned a term loan of Rs 34 lakh and thereafter applied for working capital and is sanctioned in the form of cash credit for Rs 14 lakh, ranging to a total limit of Rs 48 lakh. They have generated employment for 15 people with another 30 indirect employment. Infrastructure is completed, machines are installed .This industry is new in that area .Thus, marketing is not a problem as the bricks are relatively cheap and high in demand. Read More Shubhavi Enterprises. MAHARASHTRA / CHHATRAPATI SAMBHAJINAGAR Online Examination Centre Go Online Go Digital Mrs. Saee Kaustubh Hastak established a new unit by the name M/s Shubhavi Enterprises for conducting all types of Compititive Examinations which are nowadays being conducted through Online Mode. She approached the Bank for Financial Assistance for Setting up of online examination centre at Aurangabad. The Bank suggested to apply under Stand Up India Scheme & she readily accepted the same. This would be the first such Centre in Aurangabad ,engaged in Online Examinations. At one instance approximately 150 Students can appear for the Examination. Aurangabad is main center in Marathwada Region hence candidates from whole Marathwada region are expected to come to Aurangabad for attending the Compititive Examinations. The location of the Business is at Satara Parisar which is newly developing Area & also there are 2 / 3 Colleges surrounding the Location of Business. Thus a new & innovative set up was supported by SME Centre Aurangabad thereby fulfilling the dream project of our Hon’ble Prime Minister. Read More VARUN AQUAPURE INDUSTRY. ANDHRA PRADESH / Srikakulam Water RO Treatment Bringing benefits to the Village Smt. Sakalabaktula Shoba Rani is a Prop of M/s Varuna Aquapure Industry, she gained working knowledge in Water RO Treatment and established a unit by taking Term Loan of Rs. 17.75 lakhs and Working Capital of Rs. 6.00 Lakhs (Under StandUp India Scheme). With these loan proceeds she carried out construction of the unit and purchased required machinery for RO Treatment plant. Now she is supplying RO water in her entire village for cheaper price. With this project she eradicated Flourine problem in her village. She is also assembling Domestic RO Water purifiers and supplying them for reasonable price in and around her village. She, as a successful entrepreneur, now provides job opportunity for 10 other unemployed youth of her village. Read More Creative and Reliable Association. CHHATTISGARH / DURG Services (Consultany) Employer from Employee Ms.Chhaya Rani Rajput aged about 29 years is an MBA in HR marketing. After MBA she joined India infoline in 2011 and than joined Car Planet Enterprises Pvt. Ltd at Raipur as Manager HR & Marketing till November 2015. Later she planned to start her own set up of consultancy and marketing under the name and style of M/s. Creative and Reliable Association (CARA). She has been rendering services of job consultancy and acting as channel partner of telecom companies like Tata Tele Services Ltd. Recently she has also entered into a contract with M/s Idea cellular Ltd. The bank had extended financial assistance of Rs12.00 lakh for the venture under Stand up India scheme for the purchase of advanced devices and systems along with furnishing of the work place premises. Ms.Chhaya Rani Rajput has utilized meticulously the funds borrowed from bank as per projected schedule and set up a state of the art work place with latest infrastructure. She has employed more than 15 technically qualified persons who are providing services from work stations installed in her office. The scheme has enabled an employee to transform to employer and provide employment to more employees adding to the earning capacities of the nation. Read More Adaa Creation. UTTARAKHAND / Nainital Retail Sales of Garments Setting Example A partnership firm M/s Adaa Creation was constituted in October 2016 by two ladies Mrs Bhumika Agarwal and Mrs Himani Sijwali who decided to run a showroom for ladies’ wear by engaging professional tailors. For this purpose, they have taken premises on lease at prime locality within Walkway Mall on Nainital Road, Haldwani. The partners contacted OBC and submitted their proposal for credit facility. The appraisal team convinced by the entrepreneurial skills of the partners from the conversation and considering the fact that the partners have invested on furniture and fixture at the showroom from their own sources, credit facility of Rs.12.00 lakh was sanctioned under Stand Up India scheme. Further post demonetization need of POS machine was felt by the firm to provide the additional facility to customers for making payments through digital channel, the bank provided the same at the outlet which also proved quite helpful to boost the sales of the firm. Despite being a new venture, the firm is doing very well which could be vouched from the fact that total 102 credit transactions amounting to Rs.21.16 Lac was recorded for the period Nov-2016 to Jan-2017. The firm has given employment to 8 persons. Both the partners are thankful to bank for its timely, quick and smooth credit delivery as well as installation of POS Machine. Read More TAGO Electronics LLP. UTTAR PRADESH / Ghaziabad Electronics Emerging Green Enterprise Smt. Prabha Nautiyal was a homemaker and it was her dream to contribute to the society by proving cost effective and environmental friendly illumination to household. She formed a limited liability partnership firm on 5th September 2016 along with 2 other partners viz., Sh Gurdeep Singh and Smt Mamta Taparia. Shri Gurdeep Singh is having more than 20 years of experience in electronic & electrical systems, DG Sets while Smt Mamta Taparia is in the business of designing & trading of diamond jewelry and trading of LED lights. With some initial contribution, they intended to set up a factory at Kavi Nagar Industrial Area, Ghaziabad for manufacturing of LED bulbs, lights, mobile chargers, etc. and approached bank to provide financial assistance for the same under Stand up India scheme. The borrower has estimated a net profit of Rs 3.19 Lacs from 1st year of operations ending on 31.03.2017 (3 months) and have projected it at Rs 19.80 Lacs as on 31.03.2018.The bank sanctioned a credit facilities of Rs 1.00 crore (Term loan Rs 75 lakh and WC of Rs 25 lakh) in favour of company under Stand-up India scheme on 27.10.2016. The borrower has started production in its factory and is running the same efficiently since Jan 2017. Thus the scheme not only provided the shape to the thoughts of a housewife and turned her to a entrepreneur but has also contributed towards environmental concerns along with providing livelihood to the number of people earning bread from the enterprise. Read More Sri Durga Bhavani Industries. TELANGANA / HYDERABAD Automobile Realising Bigger Dreams M/s Sri Durga Bhavani Industries is a new proprietorship established in August 2016. The Proprietress Mrs. Boyena Sridevi is a graduate in commerce and has gained experience in this line of business in finance and administration with about eight years of experience. She is assisted by her husband Mr. Boyena Ramakrishna Rao in the business, who has 22 years of experience in this line. He has got knowledge in manufacturing of Moulds, Rubber and Plastic, Jigs and Fixtures and Precision components of Automobile sector. Considering the growth prospects, the promoters have planned to establish a unit for manufacture of Rubber and Plastic Moulds, Jigs and Fixtures, all types of Precision components and Automobile components. The promoters have approached us for setting up of the new unit and requested for a Term Loan of Rs. 90.00 lakh for construction of the industrial shed and purchase of machinery along with a working capital limit of Rs. 10.00 lakh. Since the unit is a new concern and the Proprietress being a woman entrepreneur, we have processed the proposal and sanctioned a Term loan of Rs. 90.00 lacs and a Cash Credit limit of Rs. 10.00 lacs on 28.09.2016 under Stand Up India Scheme. The firm is expected to record a turnover of Rs. 20.00 lakh till March 2017 and in FY 2017-18, it is expecting a turnover of Rs. 120.00 lakh. The woman entrepreneur not only added to the growth of MSME sector but also provided employment to many people thus adding to the GDP growth of the country in many ways and has therefore justified the name given "Stand Up India" to the scheme. Read More Shubham Plastics & Dye Makers. GUJARAT / SURAT Textile and Dyeing Passion of Being an Entrepreneur Mrs. Sheeba Sebi and Mrs. Mercy Varughese had very high dreams and desire to succeed. Though they are from a middle class family, they wanted to improve the economic condition of their families and also contribute to the growth of the society. They took up the challenge to realize their dreams and learnt tailoring which was done on job work basis. Due to lack of financial support and other financial commitments, they could not be an entrepreneur. Also due to the lack of Banking Knowledge, they were unable to approach Banks for financial assistance. Incidentally, both the entrepreneurs had come to Canara Bank, Adajan Branch during Stand-up India campaign and expressed their desire for purchasing of moulding machine and starting a partnership firm for manufacturing Textiles and Dye business in the name of M/s Subham Textiles and Dye. The branch assisted them by financing under Stand-Up India Scheme within a very short period. The borrowers are now running the unit successfully and have become financially independent. Now, the unit is also providing employment to more than 10 employees. The Borrowers also recall the good gesture of Bank which has now transformed their life. Entrepreneurship Support to Women and SC & ST Communities Stand-up India Scheme facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore Apply Here 299274 Total Applications Total Amount 275321 Sanctioned Applications Sanctioned Amount 24613 Handholding Agencies 4663 HHA Requests 77 Lenders Onboarded 156312 Branches Connected What is Stand-Up India? Stand-Up India Scheme Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise. This enterprise may be in manufacturing, services, agri-allied activities or the trading sector. In case of non-individual enterprises at least 51% of the shareholding and controlling stake should be held by either an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur. Salient Features Borrower Type Loans to SC/ST and women entrepreneurs above 18 years. Enterprise Loans for setting up greenfield enterprise. Loan size Loans between ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore. Nature of Loan Composite loan (inclusive of Term Loan and Working Capital). Promoter Contribution 15% Margin money. Security Security cover under Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Stand-Up India Loans (CGFSIL). Key Elements Stand-Up India Ecosystem How to Apply Impact Stories POONGOTHAI S K. They took up the challenge to realize their dreams and learnt tailoring which was done on job work basis. Due to lack of financial support and other financial commitments, they could not be an entrepreneur. Also due to the lack of Banking Knowledge, they were unable to approach Banks for financial assistance. Incidentally, both the entrepreneurs had come to Canara Bank, Adajan Branch during Stand-up India campaign and expressed their desire for purchasing of moulding machine and starting a partnership firm for manufacturing Textiles and Dye business in the name of M/s Subham Textiles and Dye. The branch assisted them by financing under Stand-Up India Scheme within a very short period. The borrowers are now running the unit successfully and have become financially independent. Now, the unit is also providing employment to more than 10 employees. The Borrowers also recall the good gesture of Bank which has now transformed their life. × Important Information Standup India Scheme has ended on March 31st 2025. OK

analysis.overview: Stand-Up India Scheme facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise. The enterprise may be in manufacturing, services, agri-allied activities, or trading sector. For non-individual enterprises, at least 51% shareholding and controlling stake must be held by an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur. The scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship among underrepresented groups by providing financial support and handholding through designated agencies.
objectives: Facilitate bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore to SC/ST and women entrepreneurs
Support setting up of greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services, agri-allied, or trading sectors
Ensure at least one SC/ST and one woman borrower per bank branch receives support under the scheme
Promote entrepreneurship among socially disadvantaged groups through financial inclusion
Provide composite loans inclusive of term loan and working capital
Offer security cover under Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Stand-Up India Loans (CGFSIL)
Encourage handholding and support through designated Handholding Agencies (HHAs)
Monitor and report on sanctioned applications and disbursed amounts for transparency
analysis.eligibility: Borrowers must be SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs above 18 years of age. Loans are for setting up greenfield enterprises. For non-individual enterprises, at least 51% of shareholding and controlling stake must be held by either an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur. The enterprise can be in manufacturing, services, agri-allied activities, or trading sector.
analysis.benefits: Access to bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore, composite loan inclusive of term loan and working capital, promoter contribution of 15% margin money, security cover under Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Stand-Up India Loans (CGFSIL), handholding support through designated agencies, and facilitation of greenfield enterprise setup in manufacturing, services, agri-allied, or trading sectors.
analysis.financial_support: The scheme facilitates bank loans ranging from ₹10 lakh to ₹1 Crore per borrower. The loan is a composite loan inclusive of term loan and working capital. Promoters are required to contribute 15% as margin money. Security for the loan is provided under the Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Stand-Up India Loans (CGFSIL).
analysis.application_process: 1. Visit the Stand-Up India portal at https://standupmitra.in/. 2. Click on 'Apply Here' to begin the application process. 3. Approach a participating bank branch with your business proposal. 4. Ensure eligibility criteria are met (SC/ST/woman entrepreneur above 18 years, greenfield enterprise). 5. Submit required documents including identity proof, business plan, and project details. 6. Bank processes the loan application under the Stand-Up India Scheme. 7. Upon approval, loan is sanctioned and disbursed. 8. Utilize handholding support from designated Handholding Agencies (HHAs) if needed.
required_documents: Proof of SC/ST or woman entrepreneur status (if applicable)
Any additional documents as required by the lending bank
analysis.implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry
caveats: Loans are only for greenfield enterprises (new ventures)
At least 51% shareholding and control must be held by SC/ST/woman entrepreneur in non-individual enterprises
Security is covered under CGFSIL, but terms are subject to bank and guarantee scheme guidelines
Loan amount is capped at ₹1 Crore per borrower
